clusters.title: Debugging Tauri paste-back failures due to macOS Accessibility permissions and local signing
clusters.summary: A series of captures detailing debugging of a Tauri paste-back feature failure on macOS, tracing the issue to Accessibility permissions not being granted for an ad-hoc signed app, requiring local signing setup and user guidance.
keyPoints: Paste-back feature fails with 'Accessibility permission not granted' error.
Ad-hoc signed builds lose Accessibility trust after reinstall.
Local signing setup (pnpm macos:setup-local-signing) is required.
UI should guide users to grant permission and reopen the signed app.
Permission state is tied to the signing identity, not just the app code.
A relevant GitHub issue (#10627) was referenced.
clusters.reason: All six captures are directly related to debugging a specific Tauri/macOS permission issue for the paste-back feature. They form a coherent narrative about the problem, its cause (signing/trust), and potential solutions, perfectly aligning with the existing 'Tauri macOS Permissions' topic.

possibleTopics.reason: Direct match. All captures are about macOS permissions, signing, and Accessibility trust for Tauri apps.

Captures
capture_1:
id: cap_pasteback_001
content_kind: plain_text
captured_at: 2026-04-19T14:31:00Z
source: clipboard
source_app_name: VS Code
preview: paste_back failed: Accessibility permission not granted for current signed app.
raw_text:
  paste_back failed: Accessibility permission not granted for current signed app. The event succeeds only after re-authorizing the installed bundle.

capture_4:
id: cap_pasteback_004
content_kind: plain_text
captured_at: 2026-04-19T14:39:00Z
source: clipboard
source_app_name: Notion
preview: Ad-hoc signed builds can lose Accessibility trust after reinstall.
raw_text:
  Ad-hoc signed builds can lose Accessibility trust after reinstall. We should validate paste-back only on the locally signed build that matches the installed bundle identity.

capture_5:
id: cap_pasteback_005
content_kind: plain_text
captured_at: 2026-04-19T14:42:00Z
source: clipboard
source_app_name: Slack
preview: UI should explain: grant Accessibility, then reopen the signed app before retrying.
raw_text:
  UI should explain: grant Accessibility, then reopen the signed app before retrying. Otherwise testers think paste-back is flaky when the real problem is trust state.

capture_6:
id: cap_pasteback_006
content_kind: plain_text
captured_at: 2026-04-19T14:45:00Z
source: clipboard
source_app_name: VS Code
preview: AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions only becomes true for the trusted installed bundle.
raw_text:
  AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions only becomes true for the trusted installed bundle. Permission debugging has to be tied to signing identity, not just app code.

Important output rules
1. Output valid JSON only.
2. Use only sourceIds from the captures above.
3. Do not repeat sourceIds across clusters.
4. Assign every sourceId exactly once.
5. Use null when topic suggestion is unavailable.
6. Include missingContext and possibleTopics arrays even when empty.
